nativeRange.habitat: Subtidal and intertidal rocky reef; attaches via holdfast to hard substrate in warm tropical seas.

decorPreferences: hard substrate for holdfast attachment — wedge or attach with zip-tie/fishing line; do not bury in sand

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that may graze macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Lime Green Codium listing image from Mosaic Macros — shows the paler, compact dwarf Codium form. Preferred over the generic placeholder on the Stout listing.
Mosaic Macros Stout Dwarf Codium product page image — may be a generic placeholder; verify before use.

summary: Stout Dwarf Codium is a compact, spongy green macroalgae with short, thick finger-like branches. A warm-water Codium sp., it tends to thrive in standard reef parameters and generally suits display tanks well.
careNotes:
Stout Dwarf Codium is a dwarf-form Codium sp. sold by Mosaic Macros in two color variants: a darker, more compact 'Stout Dwarf' and a paler 'Lime Green' form. Both are considerably smaller than the typical aquarium-trade Codium, reportedly reaching roughly 2–8 cm. The Mosaic listing notes a 'much shorter, compact structure than most Codium' in higher flow and 'tight branches in lower flow,' suggesting moderate to lower flow is generally advisable for specimen health.

Despite belonging to the order Bryopsidales alongside calcified genera such as Halimeda and Penicillus, Codium tissue is spongy and NOT calcified — no calcium supplementation is required for growth directly. Standard reef chemistry is generally sufficient: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, nitrate 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm (per Mosaic Macros care guide).

Lighting: full-spectrum LED is recommended; actinic-only spectrum has been noted to be insufficient for more demanding macroalgae. Medium intensity has been reported as suitable for Codium.

Attachment: should be attached to hard substrate via wedging, zip-tie/fishing line, or superglue. Do NOT bury the base in sand — unlike Caulerpa and calcareous greens, Codium is not a sand-bed genus and may melt at the base if buried. Weekly dosing of iron and iodine trace elements is generally advised by Mosaic Macros for all macroalgae.

Cross-reference with sibling species: Codium fragile ('Dead Man's Fingers', sw-algae-032) is a cool-water species preferring 10–24°C and distinctly different from this warm-water Codium sp. Do not use C. fragile as a care reference for this entry.
breedingNotes: In aquarium settings, fragmentation is the practical propagation method. Frond fragments can generally establish on hard substrate when placed in moderate flow with adequate lighting.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros — Dwarf Stout Codium
primary.notes: Primary trade source. Confirms compact dark-form Codium sp. identity, short finger-like branch morphology, flow response, and 1–2" cutting/golf-ball clump sizing. Mosaic is the sole aquarium trade source identified for this entry.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Confirms warm reef parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), full-spectrum lighting requirement, propagation methods, and sand-bed melt warning for non-Caulerpa/non-calcareous green genera. Meets 2-source minimum for this Mosaic-only entry.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Lime Green Codium (secondary listing)
additional.notes: Second Mosaic SKU consolidated into this entry. Describes a paler, smaller Codium sp. form; confirms genus-level identification and that both forms are distinct from typical full-sized Codium.
